title: Business organization
description: An exercise providing students with an opportunity to demonstrate their understanding of business organization.

assessment_items.question_content:
Producers determine their business organization based on the costs and benefits involved.

**Which type of business organization best matches the listed costs and benefits?**

Costs | Benefits
- | -
Unlimited liability for debts | Full control over business decisions
Limited ability to raise capital | Easy to start & simplified tax reporting
Personal assets at risk | Retention of all profits

[[☃ radio 1]]

Producers determine their business organization based on the costs and benefits involved.

**Which type of business organization best matches the listed costs and benefits?**

 Costs | Benefits
 - | -
 Shared liability | Combined skills and resources
 Potential for conflict| Relatively easier access to capital
 Shared profits | Shared responsibilities

[[☃ radio 1]]

Brianna and Jacobo are considering starting a business together. They are discussing forming a limited liability partnership (LLP).

**Why might they choose an LLP over a general partnership?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They want to enjoy shared decision-making but also want unlimited personal liability for the actions of any partner.
They prefer a simple formation process and fewer ongoing reporting requirements.
They want to limit their liability to their own actions and investment in the business, while also benefiting from the partnership's profit.
They prefer to distribute profits based on the size of each partner's investment.

assessment_items.question_content:
Producers determine their business organization based on the costs and benefits involved.

**Which type of business organization best matches the listed costs and benefits?**

**Costs** | **Benefits**
- | -
More complex and costly to set up | Limited liablility for shareholders
Double taxation | Easier access to capital through stock issuance
Increased government regulation | Potential for political power through lobbying

[[☃ radio 1]]

Producers determine their business organization based on the costs and benefits involved.

**Which type of business organization best matches the listed costs and benefits?**

**Costs** | **Benefits**
- | -
Initial fee paid to a particular brand and ongoing royalties | Established brand recognition
Limited control over business operations | Proven business model and support
Compliance with brand's rules and guidelines | Training and marketing support from the brand

[[☃ radio 1]]

Producers determine their business organization based on the costs and benefits involved.

**Which type of business organization best matches the listed costs and benefits?**

**Costs** | **Benefits**
- | -
Potential for slower decision making due to member voting | Member owned and operated, with shared benefits
Limited ability to raise capital | Focus on member needs and interests
Complex organizational structure| Potential tax advantages

[[☃ radio 1]]

There are different benefits to the various types of business organizations. 

**Match the benefit to the correct type of business.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: Only sole proprietors have full control over their business.
Only corporations offer their owners no personal liability
Franchises have the benefit of operating under an established brand.

Sole proprietorship: Full control

Corporation: No personal liability

Partnership: Shared responsibility

Franchise: Brand recognition

assessment_items.question_content:
There are different costs to different forms of business organizations.

**Match the cost to the correct type of business.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: Corporations are highly regulated by the government.
Franchise owners lack control over a lot of business decisions since they're operating under an established brand.
Sole proprietors have unlimited personal liability for their businesses.

Sole Proprietorship: Unlimited personal liability

Partnership: Shared profits

Corporation: Highly regulated

Franchise: Lack of control over business direction

right: Lack of control over business direction

assessment_items.question_content:
There are different costs and benefits in different forms of business organization.

**Match the costs and benefits to the correct type of business.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: The main cost of a corporation is that they are very highly regulated by the government.
A benefit of a sole proprietorship is that the owner does not have to share their profits.
The main cost of a sole proprietorship is unlimited personal liability.

Sole Proprietorship COST: Unlimited personal liability

Sole Proprietorship BENEFIT: No sharing of profits

Corporation COST: Highly regulated

Corporation BENEFIT: No personal liability

assessment_items.question_content:
A group of three friends are considering starting a gourmet burger restaurant. They all want to participate equally in running the business and making decisions. They agree that each will invest the same amount of money into the business and share the profits equally.

**Considering their needs and preferences, which form of business organization should they choose?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Rhia is considering opening a hair-cutting salon. She is unsure if she should open it as a franchise or sole proprietorship.

**Identify the costs and benefits associated with the two alternatives.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: A cost of a sole proprietorship is that the owner has unlimited liability for debts.
A benefit of a franchise is that it comes with a proven business model and support.
A cost of a franchise is that the owner has limited control over business operations.

Sole Proprietorship COST: Unlimited liability for debts

Sole Proprietorship BENEFIT: Full control control over business decisions

Franchise BENEFIT: Comes with a proven business model and support

Franchise COST: Limited control over business operations

assessment_items.question_content:
There are different costs and benefits associated with various forms of business organization.

**Match the costs and benefits to the correct type of business.**

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: A cost of a corporation is that they are more complex and costly to set up.
A benefit of a franchise is that they come with training and marketing support from the franchisor.
A cost of a franchise is that the owner has  limited control over business operations.

Corporation COST: More complex and costly to set up

Corporation BENEFIT: Potential for political power through lobbying

Franchise COST: Limited control over business operations

Franchise BENEFIT: Training and marketing support from the franchisor

right: more complex and costly to set up
potential for political power through lobbying
training and marketing support from franchisor
